rag_rules: Latched expressions must appear only in component implementations, not in type scopes or invalid contexts.
Binary expressions requiring boolean types must have valid boolean operands; avoid '<error>' type mismatches by ensuring proper expression resolution.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions resolve to valid types; unresolved identifiers lead to '<error>' types and invalid guarantees.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in annex expressions to prevent type resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference visible ports or declared helpers; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
When using conditional logic in guarantees, ensure all branches return consistent types, especially boolean.
anti_patterns: Do not place latched expressions in component types; they are only valid in implementations.
Avoid using unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations that result in '<error>' type errors.
